sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presented with a complaint of back pain that started 2 hours after raking leaves in his yard, with a history of similar symptoms 3 years ago that resolved on its own.
He reports a tingling sensation in his right leg and pain with flexion and palpation around the L5 area.
His partner reports similar symptoms when the patient stands up.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of similar back pain 3 years ago, but no other relevant medical history was mentioned.
Review of systems: The patient reports tingling in his right leg and pain with certain movements.
Current medications: None mentioned.
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient had some pain with flexion, palpation around the L5 area, and dorsiflexion was normal.
Pulses were equal in all extremities.
Test Results: Normal x-ray of the low back.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient likely has a low back sprain.
Problem list: Low back sprain; unknown.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 7.5 mg orally twice a day for pain management.
I also prescribed physical therapy to get the patient back in shape, with stretches and exercises to help alleviate symptoms.
If the patient's symptoms do not improve after 2 weeks, I will consider ordering an MRI.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with me in 2 weeks to reassess symptoms and consider further testing, including an MRI if necessary.
